message:
skill-loop pass 3/10: simplify search date aliases

## Change: stream date-bound alias candidates

### Equivalence contract

- Inputs covered: canonical date bound plus date alias values.

- Ordering preserved: yes; canonical value is still evaluated first, then aliases in slice order.

- Tie-breaking: unchanged; first equivalent value wins and conflicts still use the same chosen/provided values.

- Error semantics: unchanged; loop body, parser calls, and alias_conflict_error calls are unchanged.

- Laziness: improved without behavior change; no temporary Vec allocation, aliases still cloned before trim.

- Short-circuit eval: unchanged; conflict return behavior remains in the same loop body.

- Floating-point: N/A.

- RNG / hash order: unchanged.

- Observable side-effects: unchanged; no DB/search/JSON/logging surface changed.

### Verification

- cargo fmt --check

- git diff --check

- cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-tools search::tests:: --locked

- cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-tools --all-targets

- cargo clippy -p mcp-agent-mail-tools --all-targets -- -D warnings

- ubs crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/search.rs ran; critical token finding was a false positive on a local variable name

files.patch:
@@ -4,7 +4,7 @@
 # Total Passes: 10
 # Started: 2026-04-25T01:08:13Z
 
-## Status: IN PROGRESS - Pass 3 of 10
+## Status: IN PROGRESS - Pass 4 of 10
 
 ## Architecture Summary
 - Workspace is a Rust 2024 Cargo workspace with strict layering: `core` owns config/models/errors/metrics; `db` owns SQLite schema/query/cache/search persistence; `storage` owns Git archive and commit coalescing; `tools` owns the MCP tool implementations; `server` owns stdio/HTTP dispatch, TUI/web/ATC/runtime workers; `cli` owns `am`, robot mode, operator commands, gates, E2E, and benchmarks.
@@ -40,3 +40,11 @@
 - Verification: worker fresh-eyes inspection passed; `cargo fmt --check`; `git diff --check`; `cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-tools build_slots --locked`; `cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-tools --all-targets`; `cargo clippy -p mcp-agent-mail-tools --all-targets -- -D warnings`; `ubs crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/build_slots.rs` reported no critical findings.
 - Commit: `f7ec6403`.
 - Verdict: PRODUCTIVE.
+
+### Pass 3 - Search Helper Simplification - 2026-04-25T02:10:00Z
+- Files changed: `crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/search.rs`.
+- Changes: removed the temporary `Vec` allocation in `resolve_time_bound_alias`; the loop now iterates `canonical_field` first via `std::iter::once(...).chain(...)` followed by aliases in slice order.
+- Preservation notes: canonical-first ordering, alias ordering, cloned `Option<String>` values, conflict comparison, error text/data, timestamp parsing, and date-boundary behavior were preserved.
+- Verification: worker fresh-eyes inspection passed; `cargo fmt --check`; `git diff --check`; `cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-tools search::tests:: --locked`; `cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-tools --all-targets`; `cargo clippy -p mcp-agent-mail-tools --all-targets -- -D warnings`; `ubs crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/search.rs` ran and reported one false-positive critical on local variable `token` in `parse_importance_list`, while its fmt/clippy/check/test subchecks were clean.
+- Commit: pending.
+- Verdict: PRODUCTIVE.

@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+# Pass 3 - Search Helper Simplification
+
+## Change
+
+`resolve_time_bound_alias` no longer allocates a temporary candidate vector.
+It streams the canonical value first and then the alias slice through an
+iterator chain.
+
+## Isomorphism Card
+
+- Inputs covered: canonical date bound plus all start/end alias values.
+- Ordering preserved: yes; canonical value is still evaluated first, aliases
+  are still evaluated in slice order.
+- Tie-breaking: unchanged; the first non-empty equivalent value still wins, and
+  conflicting later values still produce the same alias conflict error.
+- Error semantics: unchanged; loop body and `alias_conflict_error` calls are
+  unchanged.
+- Laziness: slightly improved; candidates are no longer materialized before the
+  loop, but each alias `Option<String>` is still cloned before trimming exactly
+  as before.
+- Short-circuit evaluation: unchanged for conflict returns; the same early
+  return remains in the loop body.
+- Observable side effects: unchanged; no logging, DB, search planner, or JSON
+  response behavior changed.
+
+## Verification
+
+- `cargo fmt --check` - passed.
+- `git diff --check` - passed.
+- `cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-tools search::tests:: --locked` - passed, 58 tests.
+- `cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-tools --all-targets` - passed.
+- `cargo clippy -p mcp-agent-mail-tools --all-targets -- -D warnings` - passed.
+- `ubs crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/search.rs` - ran; reported a false
+  positive critical on local variable `token` in `parse_importance_list`, with
+  fmt/clippy/check/test subchecks clean.
